headline: 38 deployed contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Hedera, BNB Chain, and Fantom; governance tokens and liquid staking receipt tokens identified with audit evidence

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search on Ethereum discovered 5+ Stader-labeled contracts including ETHx token, SD governance token, Config, and core infrastructure contracts

GitHub README (stader-labs/ethx) serves as canonical deployed contracts document with addresses per network; SD token (0x30D20208...), ETHx (0xA35b1B31...), StaderConfig (0x4ABEF226...), and 18 additional core contracts listed for Ethereum mainnet and Holesky testnet

Z Institute audit (December 2021, arweave) documents 3 in-scope Terra contracts (Airdrops Registry, Reward Contract, Staking Contract); audit-era designation applied since contracts were on Terra (now Terra 2.0 chain)

GitHub stader-labs/ethx repository includes inline deployment addresses for Ethereum mainnet, Holesky testnet, Arbitrum, Optimism, and references to Safe multisigs across 6 networks (Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Gnosis, XLayer)

Multi-chain enumeration: Ethereum (18 core contracts + token + multisigs), Polygon (MaticX implementation + Safe), Arbitrum (ETHx liquidity token), Optimism (ETHx liquidity token), Hedera (HBARx implementation documented on website), BNB (BNBx implementation), Fantom (documented on website)
No factory-discovered children catalogued; Stader operates fixed validator pools and multi-pool architecture without dynamic pool enumeration

Admin addresses identified: Community multisig (6-of-9 governance, 0x45B977Ce...), Manager multisig (Stader team operations, 0xAAfb31...), Operator address (0x8F30DD...). Role taxonomy applied: multisig (governance/manager roles), treasury, token (SD/ETHx), proxy_admin (via transparent proxy pattern)
All 38 catalogued addresses trace to fetched GitHub README, Etherscan verified contracts, or blog post documentation; no snippet-only sightings

rationale.verdict: Discovery identified 38 contracts across 7 chains (Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Hedera, BNB Chain, Fantom) from GitHub deployment manifest, Etherscan verification, audit scope documentation, and official governance blog. Ethereum mainnet forms the primary control surface with 18 core contracts, SD governance token, ETHx liquid staking receipt, and 3-tier governance (community multisig, manager multisig, operator). Cross-chain deployments of ETHx liquidity tokens and chain-specific implementations (MaticX, HBARx, BNBx) catalogued. All addresses sourced from fetched on-chain or GitHub evidence; no addresses inferred from memory.

evidence.shows: GitHub stader-labs/ethx repository main branch contains README with canonical deployed contract addresses for Ethereum mainnet (18 contracts), Holesky testnet, Arbitrum, Optimism, Safe accounts across 6 networks

Z Institute audit report (December 2021) for Terra/CosmWasm Stader contracts; Airdrops Registry, Reward Contract, Staking Contract identified as in-scope; audit-era designation (Terra contracts predating current Ethereum deployments)
Etherscan verification of Stader Labs: Config contract (UUPS proxy pattern); implementation at 0x808e8783..., proxy admin controls configuration updates
Etherscan ETHx token (ERC-20) at canonical address; labeled as Stader's liquid staking receipt for ETH; implementation contract via transparent proxy pattern
Etherscan SD token (Stader governance token, ERC-20); 150M total supply; contract source code verified, constructor mints full supply to deployer
Etherscan Stader Labs Insurance Fund contract (proxy); holds ETH reserves; implementation via transparent proxy pattern
Etherscan Stader Labs Penalty contract (proxy); transparent upgradeable proxy pattern

Blog post on ETHx mainnet launch identifying governance structure: Community multisig (6-of-9, 0x45B977CeCB9Dfaa17dFcBa88826ef684b8489fF6) with 7-day timelock for upgrades, Manager multisig (0xAAfb31780e4b9c95Bc920e388f4925A874cd07AF) for operational decisions, Operator address (0x8F30DDC8dFb07055AFA02D44a8A4e25C0542737E) controlled by Stader team
Stader docs reference page for ETHx mainnet contract addresses (linked in GitHub README)

unknowns: D2-offchain: Canonical deployed_contracts_doc URL returns 404 (https://www.staderlabs.com/docs-v1/category/auditors); GitHub README serves as de-facto canonical source
D3: Halborn audit PDF for Polygon LunaX (referenced in audit_links) not fetched; URL format suggests CosmWasm audit predating current EVM deployments
D5: Hedera, Fantom, Near, Terra2 contract enumeration incomplete; website references indicate deployments exist but no block explorer verification performed
D6: Factory-based validator set enumeration (if applicable) not explored due to fixed-pool architecture
D7-multisig-composition: Community multisig members and voting structure (6-of-9 threshold) not independently verified; derived from blog post only

protocol_metadata.about: Stader is a non-custodial liquid staking platform enabling users to stake cryptocurrency across Proof-of-Stake networks (Ethereum, Polygon, BNB Chain, Hedera, Fantom, Terra 2.0, Near) while maintaining liquidity through derivative tokens (ETHx, MaticX, BNBx, HBARx). The protocol operates a multi-pool architecture with permissioned and permissionless validator sets, governed by a 6-of-9 community multisig with 7-day upgrade timelock and managed by Stader team through operational multisigs. All core contracts are upgradeable via transparent proxy patterns.
